beats.activity: Wake up, feed dog, make coffee, check weather and work calendar
beats.reasoning: Early riser wakes at 6:45am on a sunny Monday; full energy means a brisk morning routine before the commute to Bellevue.

beats.activity: Drive to Bellevue, navigate around Light Rail closure detour
beats.reasoning: Light Rail is closed 9am–3pm; commuting now before the closure avoids delays and gets her to the office with time to spare.

beats.activity: Landscape architecture work: review park designs, client calls, site planning
beats.reasoning: Morning work block; meticulous planner tackles design work early when energy is highest; breaks before the noon FIFA event.

beats.activity: Lunch and watch USA vs Wales FIFA match at a downtown bar
beats.reasoning: The 11am match is already underway; Olga stops work at noon to catch the second half at a downtown bar—casual interest in the World Cup event.

beats.activity: Walk back to office, debrief match with colleagues
beats.reasoning: Post-match conversation with coworkers; values connection but keeps it brief before returning to work tasks.

beats.activity: Afternoon landscape work: presentations, client emails, project updates
beats.reasoning: Focused afternoon work block; Light Rail closure ends at 3pm, so commute options open up again; stays until typical end of workday.

beats.activity: Drive home from Bellevue via I-405/SR-99, sunny evening light
beats.reasoning: End of workday commute; Light Rail is now operational again but driving offers flexibility; enjoys the scenic evening drive on a sunny day.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner, take dog for a walk around the neighborhood
beats.reasoning: Home meal prep and dog time; meticulous planner values routine; sunny evening is perfect for a neighborhood walk with her companion.

beats.activity: Paint in home studio, unwind after work; Light reading, tea, settle in with dog
beats.reasoning: Free time on painting; meticulous planner balances work intensity with creative outlet; wind-down activity in home before sleep.
8.5-hour overnight sleep; early riser prepares for another full day; sleep beat properly spans midnight and ends before 6am next day.

diary: Monday morning started bright—coffee tasted better than usual, and the dog practically vibrated with excitement during our walk. The Light Rail closure threw me for a loop on the drive to Bellevue, but the detour gave me time to think through the Meridian Park redesign. Work was solid; we nailed a client presentation about native plantings. Around noon, the whole office seemed to empty out for the USA-Wales match at that bar on Main Street. I'm not usually one for soccer, but the energy was contagious—everyone cheering, the sun pouring through the windows. Grabbed lunch and got swept up in it all. Drive home was golden hour perfect, I-405 practically glowing. Dinner with the dog, then I lost myself in the studio for a couple hours with my paints. Something about the blue I mixed today felt right. Ended the day peaceful.
thought_process: The light rail closure initially irritated me—I hate unexpected detours—but it actually gave my mind space to work through some design challenges I'd been stuck on. The FIFA watch was an impulse; normally I'd eat at my desk, but the office buzz was infectious and honestly, I needed the break. The sunny weather put me in a good mood that carried through the afternoon, made the presentations feel more energized. By evening, I was ready to decompress. The painting studio is where I process things, and after a solid day of client work, I needed that creative outlet. It's a rhythm I've built over years—work hard, then create for myself.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
